arms_mandatory_baselines: random-mathlib-1000-declaration-sample (the null denominator); Metamath-only identity is the standing fallback, not an arm

dependent_vars.definition: of the 1,000 random Mathlib declarations, those inside the mintable fragment
dependent_vars.name: n_in_fragment

dependent_vars.definition: verified LLM locations correct in top-5 on the math-v0 overlap set
dependent_vars.name: n_location_top5

dependent_vars.definition: LLM candidates verified by bridge F
dependent_vars.name: n_f_verified

n_planned.note: location targets = the 39 math-v0 concepts (data/math-v0 conceptCount); existing 70-record math-lean-sample (mathlib commit-pinned) is the extractor's reference, not the sample

endpoints.test: fragment rate vs the 1% gate by one-sided exact binomial over the 1,000-declaration sample, α=0.05; Wilson 95% lower bound must clear 0.01
top-5 location rate vs the 80% gate, one-sided α=0.05, Wilson lower bound over the 39-target overlap set; detectable alternative: true rate ≳0.92 (tight — printed per the P8 §1.6 decidability lint)
deterministic: every round-trip reaches the fixed point
F-verification rate of LLM candidates; near-zero (<0.01) kills the bridge programme (NF3)

extrapolation_envelope_verbatim: P1 §4b row HS2–HS8 (verbatim): Measured range: R0 — no host model. Reasonable extrapolation envelope: Model-scale-free (properties of the kernel formalism); the relevant axis is KERNEL size: verdicts on 54–10³ records re-checked at 10⁴–10⁵ during bulk authoring (G6/G7 re-run as regression). Literature anchor + licensing assumption: Formal-fragment properties don't vary with observer scale; only corpus composition shifts them.
kill_criterion_verbatim: P1 §4 HS8 kill criterion (verbatim): 'As NF3: below either bound ⇒ Metamath-only identity stands, Lean stays annotation-only; near-zero F-verification of LLM candidates kills the bridge programme. Tests: fragment rate vs the 1% gate by exact binomial over the 1,000-declaration sample (Wilson lower bound must clear 1%); top-5 location rate vs the 80% gate likewise (α=0.05, one-sided).'

corpus_hashes._recipe: kot-corpus-hash/1: digest = sha256 over the UTF-8 concatenation of one line per regular file under data/<corpus>/ (recursive; symlinks and directories excluded), each line '<sha256-of-file-bytes-hex>  <relpath>\n' with exactly two spaces, relpath POSIX-style ('/'-separated) relative to data/<corpus>/, lines sorted by the UTF-8 byte order of relpath; reference implementation tools/registry/corpus-pin.py
corpus_hashes.mathlib-1000-sample: PINNED-AT-INPUTS:d-ml (1,000-declaration random Mathlib crawl; ops amendment before any final run)

title: G8 — Lean-minting viability: fragment gate, verified LLM location, round-trip fixed point (HS8 / NF3)
